channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: India to Upgrade Next 5 S-400 Squadrons with Anti-Drone Enhancements, AWACS Link Integration After Operation Sindoor
item.description: Following the Defence Acquisition Council’s (DAC) clearance to procure five additional S-400 Triumf air defence squadrons from Russia, sources close to idrw.org indicate that the upcoming units will incorporate significant upgrades shaped by evolving threat perceptions and operational lessons drawn from recent engagements, including Operation Sindoor.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IAF Plans Squadron-Level Induction of Ghatak UCAV for Deep Strike and SEAD Missions
item.description: India’s indigenous Ghatak Unmanned Combat Aerial Vehicle (UCAV) program is emerging as a central pillar in the Indian Air Force’s future strike doctrine, with plans to deploy the platform across multiple squadrons and integrate it with both next-generation and legacy fighter aircraft.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Su-57 Twin-Seater for India: Russia Pushes Manned-Unmanned Teaming Fighter as 6th-Gen Bridge
item.description: India has once again been approached by Russia with a significantly reworked proposal under the Fifth Generation Fighter Aircraft (FGFA) framework, this time centered around a twin-seater variant of the Su-57 that is specifically optimized for Manned-Unmanned Teaming (MUM-T) operations. idrw.org has been told by Russian sources that this new offer goes beyond a conventional stealth fighter pitch and instead positions the aircraft as a transitional platform bridging current 5th-generation capabilities with emerging 6th-generation warfare concepts.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Does India Need a Single-Engine Stealth Fighter? Why a “Mini-AMCA” Could Be the Missing Link in IAF’s Future Force Structure
item.description: India’s fighter modernisation roadmap is steadily converging around high-end, twin-engine platforms such as the Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) and potentially even the F-35 if geopolitical conditions ever align. While these platforms promise deep-strike capability, survivability, and long-range dominance, they also raise a critical structural question: will the Indian Air Force end up with a capability gap at the lower end of the stealth spectrum by not fielding a single-engine fifth-generation fighter?
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Army Seeks AI-Based Fire Assistance System for Assault Rifles
item.description: The Indian Army is looking to significantly enhance the effectiveness of individual soldiers by seeking the development of a Fire Assistance System (FAS) for assault rifles under the DISC 14 innovation challenge. The initiative aims to improve target acquisition, accuracy, and engagement speed in modern combat scenarios where reaction time and precision are critical.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: HAL Focuses on Reducing Noise Levels in Hindustan-228 Cockpit and Cabin
item.description: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) has introduced a new DRISHTI challenge aimed at addressing a critical operational and ergonomic issue in the Hindustan-228 aircraft: noise reduction within the cockpit and cabin. The initiative reflects HAL’s continued effort to enhance crew comfort, mission effectiveness, and passenger experience while maintaining strict aerospace design constraints.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Saudi Snub Exposes Pakistani Establishment’s Hollow Arms Export Hype as $1.5 Billion Sudan Deal Vanishes Into Thin Air
item.description: The Pakistani establishment once again finds itself in an awkward spot as reports emerge that a much-hyped $1.5 billion weapons and fighter jets deal with Sudan has been placed on hold following strong objections from Saudi Arabia, its long-time benefactor and financier. Just months after Reuters excitedly reported that Pakistan was on the verge of sealing this major arms sale, complete with JF-17 aircraft, drones, and air defence systems to bolster Sudan’s embattled army, the same agency now cites Pakistani security and diplomatic sources claiming Riyadh has pulled the plug on financing and demanded the agreement be terminated.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: BEL inks pact with BMIT for advanced defence electronics, aerospace systems
item.description: Defence PSU BEL on Tuesday said it has signed an MoU with BMIT to collaborate on the design, development, and manufacturing of advanced electronic modules, subsystems, and systems for defence electronics and aerospace.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Gujarat ATS Arrests Two Over ‘Ghazwa-E-Hind’ Module Involving RDX Bomb-Making Plans, RSS-Linked Targeting
item.description: The Gujarat Anti-Terrorist Squad (ATS) has arrested two individuals in connection with an alleged online radicalisation and conspiracy case, in which discussions included references to “Ghazwa-e-Hind” and potential targeting of multiple groups, including RSS-linked individuals, officials said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pahalgam Terror Attack Changed Calculus, Deserves More Than Remembrance: US Warfare Expert
item.description: As the Pahalgam terror attack marks its first anniversary on Wednesday, the tragic moment demands more than recollection — it calls for examining the networks behind it, the strategy that enabled it, and the response that ensued. India’s post-attack decisions have already reshaped the calculus, a renowned international analyst reckoned on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: One Year Since Pahalgam, Pakistan Still Safe Haven For Terror Groups
item.description: Pakistan is trying to reshape its international image by benefiting from its perceived mediating role in US-Iran conflict. However, the reality is that Pakistan continues to operate as a breeding ground and safe haven for terrorist organisations, a report has stated.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India bats for greater Global South representation in UNSC
item.description: India has underscored the need for greater representation in the permanent membership of the UN Security Council, particularly of countries from the Global South, and called for a fair and inclusive global financial system.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Hormuz disruptions have direct implications for India: Rajnath Singh
item.description: In the backdrop of the ongoing West Asian conflict, Defence Minister Rajnath Singh on Tuesday said disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz are not distant events, they are stark realities with direct implications for India’s security and economic stability.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s crude imports fall 13% as Hormuz disruption cuts West Asia supply
item.description: India’s crude oil imports fell 13 per cent in March from pre-war levels in February, with half coming from Russia, after the US-Israeli conflict ?with Iran halted West Asian shipments via the Strait of Hormuz, ?shipping data showed.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Defence MoS Sanjay Seth To Honour Naval Civilians On April 23
item.description: Minister of State for Defence Sanjay Seth will honour naval civilians for their contributions and excellence in various fields, including enhancement of the operational preparedness of the Indian Navy, at an event on April 23, an official said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: admin
